content.decision_question: Should Hungary prioritize building a reconciled monitoring architecture before pursuing structural or finance reform, or pursue them in parallel given that data alone will not change access?
content.evidence_boundary: Decomposition analysis can clarify how much of the SNI/BTMN rise is attributable to definitional change, diagnostic practice, or true prevalence, and can establish an auditable baseline. It cannot determine whether political actors will act on inconvenient findings, nor can it substitute for a decision about whether access should remain diagnosis-gated.
content.tension: Building a reconciled measurement architecture (TP-live-t2) can reveal how much identification growth reflects diagnostic-practice, incentive, or definitional artifacts rather than true need, but by itself it leaves the sole gatekeeper and categorical entitlement structure untouched, meaning transparency does not translate into changed access unless paired with structural or finance reform.
content.title: Diagnostic visibility versus classification-linked access
